items.title: Split the simulation-heavy test suite into actionable shards
items.insight: The known 20-minute local timeout is not just slowness; it hides which football surface failed. Sharding by contract, runtime, simulation realism, and Studio protocol turns a stalled monolith into coachable signals for local agents and CI.
items.recipe: Add a shard runner with named test groups, npm scripts for each shard, and a CI matrix that runs shards independently.

items.title: Make the GitHub Pages deploy prove the playable client boots
items.insight: The project has a good static smoke script, but deploy only built artifacts and uploaded them. The deploy gate should prove the client-only league path actually boots before publishing a public game surface.
items.recipe: Run the existing static Pages smoke test in deploy-pages after build and wire CI to execute the same smoke gate after the Pages bundle is produced.

items.title: Give future agents a low-token map from change surface to test shard
items.insight: Agents currently have to grep the suite or rerun too much to choose a verification path. A compact contract map cuts repeated context and command cost by telling the next agent exactly which shard covers browser runtime, sim realism, protocol helpers, or UI smoke.
items.recipe: Document shard ownership in the implementation plan and package scripts, then log the measurement plan for full-suite timeout replacement.

evidence: npm test passed with 131 default tests across core/runtime/sim-contract/sim-realism/studio shards
npm run test:long passed with 3 long-smoke tests
.github/workflows/ci.yml browser-gates job runs build:pages and smoke:pages
docs/IMPLEMENT_PLAN.md records shard ownership and measurement plan
scripts/run-test-shard.mjs list support documents file membership
Long-horizon realism/determinism checks isolated behind npm run test:long and npm run test:full
